hypothesis: If language models for materials design are fine-tuned using Direct Preference Optimization with rewards derived from physics-based thermodynamic simulations, then they will generate BCC/B2 superalloy compositions with higher thermodynamic validity and reward scores compared to supervised fine-tuning alone, few-shot prompting of large API models, and agentic baselines, because preference learning directly optimizes the model's generative distribution toward compositions satisfying multi-objective synthesis constraints that cannot be captured by supervised imitation of known examples alone.
